typography.hierarchy_rules: Use large prominent text (text-xl to text-2xl) for the Next Appointment time to create a 'Next Best Action' focal point. Ensure high-contrast. Never default to generic sans for large focal numbers if a display font is available.

colors.primary: Use existing CutSync colors.brand (#F5A524) for the active accent on the Next Appointment Card.
colors.background: colors.surface (#FFFFFF) or colors.surfaceRaised (#F9FAFB) based on tokens.

states.loading: Use colors.canvasSoft or a skeleton pulse with colors.border
states.free: colors.canvasSoft with colors.textMuted to indicate empty state. Vibe: approachable, calm.
states.error: colors.dangerSoft for background, colors.danger for text

colors.rules: Avoid generic SaaS Blue. Apply the 'Tinted Grey' rule implicitly by using existing theme tokens.

visual enhancers.shadows: Use atmosphericShadow and elevated shadow styles for the Next Appointment Card. Mix soft shadows with crisp inner borders.
visual enhancers.borders: 1px hairline border (colors.hairline) with radii.lg for structural clarity.
visual enhancers.empty_states: Do not leave a blank space. If there is no appointment, show a clear 'Agenda Livre' state with a friendly icon. Empty spaces feel like a bug; make them feel intentional.

grids.layout_mode: High Density Grid (For Dashboards / Analytics) modified for Mobile-First.
grids.rules: Grid items must be direct children. The Next Appointment Card spans full width at the top of the dashboard to serve as the 'Hero' widget.

layouts.spacing: Generous Spacing Philosophy. Use p-4 to p-6 (padding 16 to 24) inside the card. Ensure distinct visual separation between the Admin's Daily Panel and the Global Next Appointment Card (Admin).
layouts.alignment: Optical alignment over geometrical alignment. Left-align text, but ensure icon spacing feels balanced. Asymmetry creates tension and interest.

motion.transitions: Add smooth opacity transitions when the Realtime update swaps the appointment data.
motion.micro_interactions: Subtle scale-down on press (if the card is actionable). Avoid default 'all' transitions.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure minimum 4.5:1 ratio. Text colors from tokens should be strictly used.
accessibility.test_ids: MUST include data-testid='next-appointment-card', data-testid='global-next-appointment-card', data-testid='next-appointment-time', data-testid='next-appointment-free', data-testid='next-appointment-error'

media: Not applicable for this specific feature.
instructions_to_main_agent: Create a shared hook `useNextAppointment.js` in `/app/src/hooks/` using Supabase Realtime to subscribe to 'appointments' table changes (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE). This ensures updates are immediate after any action.
Implement `NextAppointmentCard.js` for the professional dashboard. It must show distinct states: Loading (skeleton/spinner), Free Schedule (Agenda livre), Error, and Active Appointment.
Implement `GlobalNextAppointmentCard.js` for the Admin dashboard. This must show the very next appointment across the entire unit, remaining completely independent from the admin daily panel.
Integrate the professional card into the Barber/Professional dashboard. Replace the existing static 'Próximo atendimento' metric with this new robust, real-time component.
Integrate the admin global card into the Admin dashboard.
Preserve the strict visual system of CutSync, utilizing AppCard, StatusBadge, and tokens.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: GENERAL UI UX DESIGN GUIDELINES: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight. Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy. Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions. Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Subtle grain textures or custom cursors separate good from extraordinary. ALWAYS add data-testid attributes to interactive and informational elements to facilitate robust automated testing.
